Amended MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Attorneys for Plaintiffs, Reina Castro Jaramillo, Alejandro Bojorge, Sr., Zulma Medina, and Alexander Medinafor Plaintiff.
Ana A. Davila, Esq., KERN SEGAL & MURRAY (415) 474-1900 Attorney for Defendant, VICTOR MEJIA ARREOLA
Kenneth Brill, CSR# 12797, Esquire Solutions 415-591-3333 sanfranciscoscheduling@esquiresolutions.com Court reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VICTOR ARREOLA Amended MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Victor Mejia Arreola's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint (FAC) is denied.

Mejia's "amended notice of motion" first says "the FAC fails to allege facts sufficient to support punitive damages." (Not. 2:1-2.) I disagree. (See, e.g., FAC 6:1-16, 16:1-17:8 and rulings on Mejia's demurrers.)

The motion's other arguments are also unavailing. (Not. 2:11-3:8.) Negligence per se is not a cause of action, and, as ruled on Mejia's demurrers, the FAC adequately pleads negligence. (Id. at 2:11-26.) One misspelling is not a ground to strike. (Id. at 2:27.) As ruled on Mejia's demurrers, infliction of emotional distress is adequately pled, and it would not matter if the cited statutes were unconstitutional. (Id. at 2:28-3:8.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter:Kenneth Brill, CSR# 12797, Esquire Solutions 415-591-3333 sanfranciscoscheduling@esquiresolutions.com Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)

RE: Defendant VICTOR ARREOLA's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling - ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The Davila declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred in person or by telephone with regard to the amended complaint in compliance with CCP 430.41 and CCP 435.5. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by August 25, 2023.
